commands.review_summary:
The PR adds RSS podcast transcript URL validation, manual redirect handling, and two mocked SSRF regression tests for literal loopback and redirect-to-loopback transcript URLs.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a feed-controlled hostname such as a public-looking domain that resolves to a private address passes the PR's string-only hostname checks before runtime fetch resolves it.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer/security review and contributor real-behavior proof before merge; the repair direction is clear, but the shared guard placement and compatibility tradeoff should be approved.

target.title: [security] fix(core): guard RSS transcript fetches
target.body:
## Summary

This PR hardens the RSS podcast transcript trust boundary so feed-controlled `<podcast:transcript>` URLs cannot drive host-side transcript fetching into local or private network services.

- Fixes a server-side request forgery risk in the RSS transcript provider path.
- Validates transcript URLs before any fetch, including rejected schemes and local/private address ranges.
- Revalidates every redirect target instead of relying on automatic redirect following.
- Adds regression coverage for direct loopback transcript URLs and public-to-loopback redirects.

## Security issues covered

| Issue | Impact | Severity |
|-------|--------|----------|
| RSS podcast transcript URLs can trigger SSRF to local/private network services | A malicious or compromised feed can cause the host-side transcript extraction path to request loopback, link-local, RFC1918/private, or otherwise internal URL literals and treat the response as transcript content. | High under deployments that summarize attacker-controlled podcast/RSS URLs; Medium hardening if all feed inputs are treated as trusted/operator-controlled. |

## Before this PR

- `tryFetchTranscriptFromFeedXml()` decoded the feed-controlled transcript `url` attribute and passed it directly to the transcript fetch implementation.
- Transcript fetches used automatic redirects, so a public transcript URL could redirect into a local/private/internal host after the initial request.
- The RSS transcript tests did not lock in SSRF protection for direct loopback transcript URLs or redirect-based bypasses.

## After this PR

- RSS transcript URLs are parsed and validated before fetching.
- Non-HTTP(S) schemes and local/private/internal hostname literals are rejected before the fetch implementation is called.
- Transcript fetches use manual redirect handling, and every `Location` target is validated before the next request.
- Regression tests cover both direct loopback transcript URLs and public-to-loopback redirects.

## Why this matters

Podcast/RSS feed XML is content controlled by the feed publisher, and feed URLs may be user-supplied in deployments that summarize external podcasts. Without a URL boundary at the nested transcript-fetch layer, feed content can move the host process from normal remote content retrieval into requests against services that were only intended to be reachable from the local machine or private network.

## Attack flow

```text
attacker controls or influences a podcast/RSS feed
    -> feed includes <podcast:transcript url="http://127.0.0.1:...">
        -> RSS transcript provider decodes the nested URL
            -> host-side fetch requests the local/private target
                -> response is accepted as transcript text
```

A redirect variant follows the same path when the feed points at an apparently public transcript URL that redirects to a blocked local/private target.

## Affected code

| Issue | Files |
|-------|-------|
| RSS transcript SSRF via feed-controlled transcript URLs and redirects | `packages/core/src/content/transcript/providers/podcast/rss-transcript.ts`, `tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts` |

## Root cause

Issue: RSS podcast transcript URLs can trigger SSRF to local/private network services

- The nested transcript URL was trusted after XML extraction, even though it came from feed-controlled RSS content.
- Automatic redirect following let the final fetch target differ from the initially selected URL without another safety check.
- The provider lacked regression tests for local/private transcript URL denial.

## CVSS assessment

| Issue | CVSS v3.1 | Vector |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| RSS podcast transcript SSRF | 8.2 High | `CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:L` |

Rationale:

- The High assessment assumes a deployment where unauthenticated or low-friction users can submit attacker-controlled podcast/RSS URLs for summarization, and where the daemon or extension runs with host/network access to private services.
- Confidentiality is the primary concern because internal service responses can be fetched and treated as transcript text; availability impact is bounded to possible request pressure or redirect behavior.
- If maintainers consider all feed URLs trusted/operator-controlled, this is still useful Medium hardening for a brittle network trust boundary.

## Safe reproduction steps

### 1. Direct loopback transcript URL

1. Use RSS XML containing a `<podcast:transcript>` element whose `url` points at a loopback literal such as `http://127.0.0.1:8080/transcript.txt`.
2. Run the RSS transcript extraction path with a mocked fetch implementation.
3. Observe that vulnerable code attempts to fetch the loopback transcript URL.

### 2. Public URL redirecting to loopback

1. Use RSS XML containing a `<podcast:transcript>` URL that initially points at a public-looking HTTPS URL.
2. Have the first mocked response return a redirect `Location` pointing at a loopback literal.
3. Observe that vulnerable code follows the redirect automatically, reaching the blocked target.

The regression tests added in this PR use mocked fetches only and do not contact real local or private-network services.

## Expected vulnerable behavior

- A feed-controlled transcript URL pointing at `localhost` or loopback/private address literals can be sent to the host-side fetch implementation.
- A public transcript URL can redirect to a local/private target without redirect-target validation.
- The returned response can be accepted as transcript content by the summarization path.

## Changes in this PR

- Adds transcript URL validation before fetch.
- Rejects non-HTTP(S) transcript URL schemes.
- Blocks localhost and local/private/internal IPv4 literal ranges, including loopback, link-local, RFC1918/private, shared address space, benchmarking, multicast, and reserved ranges.
- Blocks local/private/internal IPv6 literal ranges, including unspecified, loopback, unique-local, link-local, multicast, documentation, and IPv4-mapped/compatible addresses that resolve to blocked IPv4 ranges.
- Switches transcript fetches to `redirect: "manual"`.
- Revalidates each redirect `Location` before following it.
- Caps transcript redirect handling to avoid unbounded redirect loops.
- Adds regression tests for direct loopback transcript URLs and public-to-loopback redirects.

## Files changed

| Category | Files | What changed |
|----------|-------|--------------|
| RSS transcript provider hardening | `packages/core/src/content/transcript/providers/podcast/rss-transcript.ts` | Adds URL parsing, blocked host detection, manual redirect handling, redirect revalidation, and redirect-depth limiting. |
| Security regression tests | `tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts` | Covers direct loopback transcript URL rejection and redirect-to-loopback rejection with mocked fetches. |

## Maintainer impact

- The patch is scoped to RSS podcast transcript fetching.
- Normal HTTP(S) transcript URLs on public hosts remain supported.
- Redirects still work when every redirect target remains within the allowed public HTTP(S) URL boundary.
- The main behavior change is that transcript URLs resolving to local/private/internal literal hosts, unsupported schemes, or unsafe redirect targets are skipped instead of fetched.

## Fix rationale

- The trust boundary belongs at the nested transcript URL fetch because the URL is declared by RSS feed content, not by local application code.
- Manual redirect handling is required because validating only the initial URL leaves a public-to-private redirect bypass.
- The tests exercise the security boundary with mocked fetches, so the regression signal is deterministic and does not depend on real internal services.

## Type of change

- [x] Security fix
- [x] Tests
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactor with no behavior change

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm -s test tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts` — `2` tests passed.
- [x] `git diff --check && pnpm -s typecheck` — passed.
- [x] `pnpm -s format:check` — passed; all matched files used the correct format.
- [x] `pnpm -s lint` — passed with `0` warnings and `0` errors.

Executed with:

- `pnpm -s test tests/security.rss-transcript-ssrf.test.ts`
- `git diff --check && pnpm -s typecheck`
- `pnpm -s format:check`
- `pnpm -s lint`

## Disclosure notes

- This PR is bounded to RSS podcast transcript URL fetching and redirect handling.
- The reproduction coverage is safe and mocked; no production services or real local/private-network endpoints were contacted.
- No unrelated files are changed.
